# Security Policy

This repository is a collection of **research and educational** agent use cases. It ships
synthetic data, deterministic mock backends, and eval harnesses. It contains no production
secrets, no real customer data, and no deployed service — the "secrets," "credentials," and
"attacks" in the use cases are fabricated fixtures generated by seeded RNGs.

## What counts as a vulnerability here

Because there is no live system, the relevant security issues are about the **code you run
when you reproduce a result**:

- A bug in the harness or a use case that could execute untrusted input, write outside the
  repo, or exfiltrate real environment data (e.g. mishandling an API key from your shell).
- A dependency with a known CVE pulled in by `pip install -e`.
- A scenario generator or scorer that could be coerced into unsafe behaviour by crafted
  input.

The adversarial techniques *documented inside the use cases* (prompt injection, tool
poisoning, exfiltration, malicious-artifact admission) are the subject matter, not
vulnerabilities in this repo — they run only against synthetic fixtures and the mock model.

## Reporting

- **Non-sensitive issues** (a dependency CVE, a reproducibility bug): open a normal
  [GitHub issue](https://github.com/immu4989/awesome-agentic-usecases/issues).
- **Sensitive issues** (something that could harm someone reproducing a result before it is
  patched): use GitHub's private
  [security advisory](https://github.com/immu4989/awesome-agentic-usecases/security/advisories/new)
  so it can be fixed before disclosure.

Please include how to reproduce, the affected file, and the impact. There is no bounty; this
is an open, non-commercial project, and fixes are made on a best-effort basis.

## Reproducing safely

- Real-model runs read provider API keys from your environment. The harness sends only the
  synthetic scenario text to the model backend; it never reads other environment variables
  into prompts. Still, run evals in an environment scoped to the one provider key you intend
  to use.
- The mock backend (`--backend mock`) needs no key and makes no network calls — use it to
  exercise the full pipeline at zero risk and zero cost.
